title: Out Of Time, Out Of Tune.
content:
We blame, nay, we despise her pains
    That wets her garden when it rains:
    But when the drought has dried the knot,
    Then let her use the wat'ring-pot.
    We pray for showers, at our need,
    To drench, but not to drown our seed.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Out Of Time, Out Of Tune.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
